changelog:
Comprehensive review pass — security hardening + dead-code purge + UX bugs.

Security:
- Default install is now secure: RequireApiKey: true on first-run, plus a starter API key auto-generated and logged once at startup. Operators can rotate / delete from the dashboard.
- 32 MiB MaxBytesReader on every JSON body read; WebSocket SetReadLimit(32 MiB). Stops a single client from exhausting memory with an unbounded body.
- WebSocket CheckOrigin now enforces same-origin for browser clients; non-browser clients (Codex CLI without an Origin header) still work. KIRO_WS_ALLOW_ANY_ORIGIN=1 reverts to the old permissive behaviour.
- Admin auth is header-only now (X-Admin-Password). The legacy admin_password cookie path was a CSRF vector and has been removed.
- Per-IP failure rate-limit on admin password attempts: 10 wrong attempts in 5 minutes locks the IP for the rest of the window.
- Password change now requires the current password to be supplied and verified.
- /admin/api/apikeys list endpoint masks key secrets to last 4 chars ("sk-kg-...wxyz"). Full secret is shown once at creation only — same UX as AWS/OpenAI. Lost a key? Delete and create a new one.
- HTML-escape helper added; user-controlled API key name is now escaped before innerHTML interpolation in the dashboard.

Bugs:
- apiUpdateSettings used a non-pointer struct, so the dashboard's 'change password' or 'toggle over-usage' POSTs silently set RequireApiKey=false and cleared ApiKey. Settings now use pointer fields and only patch what was supplied.
- compareVersions() couldn't rank the fork's '-A##' patch series because split('.') produced 'NaN' on the suffix. The dashboard's update notification was effectively broken between A-revisions. Fixed.
- apiGetStatus read atomic counters without atomic.Load and read totalCredits without the credits mutex. Data race on the most-frequently-polled endpoint. Fixed.
- WebSocket Responses transport was missing checkOverageError on the failure path and OnError on the streaming callback — 402 OVERAGE upstream errors didn't auto-disable AllowOverage. Fixed.
- Stale i18n: analytics.byModelHint claimed 'resets on restart' (was true pre-A14, false now). Updated to reflect SQLite persistence in zh + en.
- Duplicate i18n keys: settings.enableApiKey was defined twice in each language table (the second definition silently shadowed the first). Removed.

Dead code purge:
- proxy/modelstats.go (in-memory map, snapshotModelStats, recordModelUsage, resetModelStats) deleted entirely. The endpoint that consumed it was rewritten in A14 to read from SQLite; the writer functions were still firing on every request but nothing read the data.
- 7 recordModelUsage call sites removed (4 in handler.go, 2 in responses_handler.go, 1 in responses_ws.go).
- 1 resetModelStats call removed from apiResetStats.
- Empty if-block in responses_ws.go removed.

Docs:
- README env-var table now lists LOG_LEVEL, KIRO_ALLOW_DEFAULT_PASSWORD, KIRO_WS_ALLOW_ANY_ORIGIN, DATA_DIR, RUN_UID/RUN_GID.

Deferred (would require restructure or contradict 'no over-engineering'):
- Save() debouncing in ConsumeAPIKey (durability semantics change).
- TOCTOU race in CheckAPIKeyLimit + ConsumeAPIKey (acknowledged, small-scale impact).
- Test coverage for new modules (would balloon scope).
- Splitting cfgLock into per-domain mutexes (architectural).

A16: real per-key limit enforcement + /v1/responses tracking.
A15: legacy fast-path tracking gap + datetime-local roundtrip.
A14: persistent SQLite statistics.
A13: highly customizable api keys.
A12: unify api keys.
A11: WebSocket transport.
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
